_meta.author: refine-scorecard skill (Claude)
_meta.note: Evidence-based finalization of Demi Busatta Cabrera (FL HD-114, R). Replaces party-default archetype with cited roll-call evidence. KEY DEVIATION: voted NAY on HB 1557 (Parental Rights in Education / 'Don't Say Gay') 2/24/2022 with ~6 other Republicans -> biblical_marriage q4 FALSE. Otherwise reliably conservative on guns (HB 543 permitless carry YEA), immigration (SB 1718 YEA), life (HB 5 + SB 300 YEA), school choice (HB 1 YEA), bathroom bill (HB 1521 YEA). Status verified: sitting HD-114 incumbent, won 2024 reelection, running again in 2026 R primary 8/18/2026.

set.office: State Representative, FL House HD-114 (sitting; 2026 R primary candidate)

profile.background: Born Nov 4, 1990, Cape Coral, FL. B.S. Florida State University. Former chief of staff to FL Sen. Anitere Flores; nonprofit development director. In FL House since Nov 2020 (HD-114, Coral Gables/Miami-Dade).
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-based refinement 2026-06-20 (web-verified against myfloridahouse.gov/flsenate.gov roll calls, FastDemocracy, Ballotpedia, Florida Politics). Status: sitting HD-114 incumbent, won reelection 2024 (def. Bornstein), running 2026 R primary (8/18/2026). NOTE: a prior session's Freedom Index entry mis-listed HB 1557 as YEA and HB 1 (2023) as NAY; both corrected here against the official roll calls (HB 1557 = NAY, HB 1 = YEA).

demi-busatta.notes_set: FL HD-114 (Coral Gables/Miami-Dade), R, in office since Nov 2020. Won reelection 2024 over Matthew Bornstein; running again in the 2026 Republican primary (8/18/2026, general 11/3/2026). DOCUMENTED RECORD (HARD = roll calls): LIFE - voted YEA on HB 5 (2022, 15-week ban) and SB 300 (2023, 6-week Heartbeat Protection Act). MARRIAGE/SEXUALITY - voted YEA on HB 1521 (2023 bathroom/facilities-by-sex bill) BUT voted NAY on HB 1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education / 'Don't Say Gay') on 2/24/2022, one of ~6-7 House Republicans to break ranks (passed 69-47). EDUCATION/FAMILY - voted YEA on HB 1 (2023 universal school-choice vouchers, 3/17/2023, 83-27); backed the Stop WOKE Act (anti-CRT). IMMIGRATION - voted YEA on SB 1718 (2023 immigration crackdown / sanctuary-ban / E-Verify, 5/2/2023, 83-36) -- did NOT deviate despite Miami-Dade seat. GUNS - voted YEA on HB 543 (2023 constitutional/permitless carry, 3/24/2023, 76-32) and on bills lowering the firearm purchase age to 18 (HB 1543/2023, HB 1223/2024, HB 759/2025) per Freedom Index. Freedom Index (freedomindex.us) overall score 71%. KEY DEVIATION: the HB 1557 NAY is the single cross-party catch -- a Miami-Dade R who broke ranks on the marquee LGBTQ-curriculum bill. No evidence found of abortion-industry (PP/NARAL/EMILY's List) funding.

0.note: Voted YEA on SB 300 (2023 six-week Heartbeat Protection Act) and HB 5 (2022 15-week abortion ban) -- affirmative pro-life voting record.

1.note: Backed restrictions (15-wk, 6-wk) but no evidence she has voted for or advocated abortion ABOLITION (the rubric distinguishes restriction from abolition).

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on embryonic stem cell / IVF embryo discard / chimeric experimentation.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on euthanasia / assisted suicide.

4.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of abortion-industry (PP/NARAL/EMILY's List) funding; also no source affirmatively confirming she never took any, so left null rather than asserting TRUE.

0.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a public statement affirming one-man-one-woman marriage.

1.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on opposing same-sex marriage / civil unions.

2.note: Voted YEA on HB 1521 (2023 Facility Requirements Based on Sex / 'bathroom bill') 4/19/2023, 80-37 -- affirms biological sex in facility law.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted NAY on HB 1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education / 'Don't Say Gay') 2/24/2022 (passed 69-47), one of ~6-7 House Republicans to break ranks -- contrary to the rubric position of opposing LGBTQ promotion in schools.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB 1 (2023 universal school-choice/voucher expansion) 3/17/2023, 83-27.

1.note: No individually-verified vote on parental-consent-for-minor-gender-care (SB 254); not scored without confirmation.

2.note: Voted for the 'Stop WOKE Act' (anti-CRT in schools) per Ballotpedia.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a verified vote on porn age-verification (HB 3).

4.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on faith-based adoption agencies / same-sex placement.

0.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of an individually-verified CBDC vote (HB 7049).

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on usury / tithe-friendly tax structures.

2.note: Voted YEA on SB 1718 (2023) 5/2/2023, 83-36 -- the bill bars local funds for unauthorized-immigrant IDs and invalidates out-of-state licenses issued to them (anti-sanctuary). Did NOT deviate despite Miami-Dade seat.

3.note: SB 1718 (YEA) expands mandatory E-Verify for employment.

4.note: No individually-verified vote on SB 264 (foreign farmland ownership); not scored without confirmation. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on birthright citizenship.

1.note: Voted YEA on bills lowering the firearm purchase age to 18 (HB 1543/2023, HB 1223/2024, HB 759/2025) -- pro-gun, but no verified direct vote opposing red-flag laws / magazine limits, so left null.
